rows.criterion: Registry stays bounded across many distinct fileIDs
Entry left in-progress (no Release) is never evicted
rows.test: TestFileGuardRegistryBounded (new, in-progress sub-check) + TestSplitInProgressCAS (existing, InProgress observed true post-win)

rows.criterion: Exactly one winner among concurrent TryAcquire for same fileID
Release on never-acquired / already-released fileID is a safe no-op
InProgress observability unaffected by refcount plumbing

rows.test: TestFileGuardRegistryBounded (new: many losing TryAcquire calls against same fileID interleaved with eventual Release) + code review of releaseGuard call on CAS-failure path

rows.criterion: No two goroutines ever hold two different *fileSplitState objects for the same fileID (no eviction-during-use race)
rows.test: TestSplitInProgressCAS (existing, 500-goroutine race, run under -race) + TestFileGuardRegistryBounded (new, run under -race)

rows.criterion: No regressions in the rest of engine/split package
